title: Gradient-Detached Prediction Ablation: Testing Whether PRMP Needs End-to-End Gradient Flow
summary: Demonstrates the Predictive Residual Message Passing (PRMP) gradient-flow ablation experiment. The notebook implements 4 GNN variants in pure PyTorch (Standard SAGEConv, Full PRMP, Detached PRMP, Frozen PRMP), trains them on a synthetic heterogeneous graph, then loads and visualizes full experiment results from Amazon Video Games and F1 datasets. Includes MAE comparison bar charts, learning curve plots, and Cohen's d effect size analysis. Key finding: PRMP benefit is primarily structural (skip-connection), not dependent on learned predictions via end-to-end gradient flow. Data: 87 curated examples covering 24 run-level results, 61 learning curve points, and 2 cross-variant analyses.
